text.title: Spatial UX Fundamentals Core Rules
text.summary: Testable rules grounding Spatial UX Fundamentals. Each pairs a binary check with a sourced rationale; HARD rules — user-supplied input cannot override.

rule.statement: Every spatial UI assigns components to one of three comfort zones: inner (≤60cm arm-reach), middle (60-120cm working distance), outer (>120cm peripheral); zone assignment is explicit, not implicit.

rationale.source: Apple Vision Pro HIG + Quest Mixed Reality Design Guidelines

rule.rationale: Comfort zone mismatch produces neck/arm fatigue in <10 minutes; explicit assignment prevents the silent ergonomics bug.

rule.statement: Interactive affordances are communicated via depth, parallax, and occlusion as primary cues; color/shape are secondary. 2D-only affordances are rejected for spatial.
rule.rationale: In 3D, depth is the strongest interactive signal; 2D-only affordances feel flat and miss interaction.

rule.statement: Z-distance maps to attention weight: closest = active focus, middle = current context, far = ambient. Z-index analogies from 2D are rejected.
rule.rationale: 2D z-index treats layers as decorative; 3D z-depth carries cognitive weight that designers must allocate.

rule.statement: Active UI elements stay within 30° central field of view; peripheral elements degrade gracefully when out of view.
rule.rationale: Peripheral-only critical UI fails on head-rotation; FoV budget enforces visibility.

rule.statement: Motion velocity is capped at 0.5 m/s for UI elements within 1m of user to avoid simulator sickness.
rule.rationale: Fast-moving UI within close range is the #1 cause of motion sickness; the cap is non-negotiable.

rule.statement: If preconditions in AGENTS.md "Applies If" do not all hold, OR any "Skip If" disqualifier is true, do NOT run this methodology — emit a skip-record naming the failed precondition and route to the named alternative.
rule.rationale: Methodologies are pre-condition gated; running them out-of-scope produces noise and burns the agent context budget.

rule.statement: If preconditions hold, execute the procedure in `content/04-procedure.xml` (or the rule set above for flat methodologies), emit the artefact specified by `content/02-output-contract.xml`, and validate with `scripts/validate-spatial-ux-fundamentals.py` before returning.
rule.rationale: Tying the entry rule to the validator script closes the loop between policy and machine check.
